aiTitle: Analyze terminal log for code anomalies

content.thinking:
The user wants me to analyze the terminal log and check if there are any anomalies in the code. Let me read the log carefully and identify potential issues.

Looking at the terminal log:

1. **BLER is very high (~0.60)** - Block Error Rate around 60% for all UEs. This is extremely high. Typical target BLER in 5G is around 10% (BLER target is usually 10% for outer-loop link adaptation). 60% BLER is abnormal.

2. **Cell throughput is low**: 42.97 Mbps with peak 136.51 Mbps - utilization is only ~31.4% of peak. 

With 64 UEs and MU-MIMO enabled, I'd expect much better throughput utilization than what we're seeing here.

3. **CSI SINR values are identical across all subbands** - e.g., `SINR=[31.9 31.9 31.9 31.9 31.9 31.9] dB` - all 6 subbands have exactly the same SINR value. This is suspicious for a subband mode. With subband PMI, different subbands should typically have different channel conditions.

4. **CQI values are identical across all subbands** - e.g., `CQI=[15 15 15 15 15 15]` - all subbands the same. This could be because the channel is flat given the CDL-B model with 100ns RMS delay at 10MHz bandwidth and 30kHz subcarrier spacing, but it's still unusual.

5. **MCS min = 0 for all UEs** - Every UE has MCS min of 0, indicating retransmissions and HARQ failures at the lowest modulation scheme, which aligns with the high BLER we're observing.

6. **Average per-UE throughput is very low** - around 0.5-1.0 Mbps per UE, which works out to roughly 0.67 Mbps/UE across the 64 users. With 128T128R and SINR values around 25-32 dB, I'd expect significantly better performance.

7. **BLER ~60% is consistently high** - This persistent error rate suggests the link is fundamentally unstable.

The link adaptation mechanism isn't responding correctly to the high BLER, or there's an issue with how HARQ failures are being processed. The MCS is staying at 25-26 despite the 60% error rate, which points to either overly optimistic CQI reports that don't reflect actual channel conditions, or the MCS selection algorithm isn't properly accounting for MU-MIMO interference. When BLER climbs this high, the link adaptation should be backing off the modulation scheme.

Looking at the grant distribution, I'm getting about 87.5 grants per UE over 50 frames across 500 slots, which seems reasonable. The layer average of 3.7-3.9 per UE is good for the 128T128R configuration. But there are some clear anomalies emerging here.

The identical SINR values across all subbands is suspicious—that looks like a computation bug where the per-subband SINR calculation isn't working and is instead falling back to a wideband value. Combined with the MCS floor hitting zero frequently due to HARQ failures, this suggests the outer-loop link adaptation either isn't enabled or the CQI-to-MCS mapping is fundamentally broken. I need to dig into the code to pinpoint where these issues are coming from. in the subband SINR calculation — that's likely where the bug is. Since the user specifically asked about code anomalies, I should examine the relevant MATLAB files to confirm whether the subband SINR is being computed correctly or just replicated from a wideband value.

content.text: Để phân tích kỹ hơn, tôi cần đọc thêm code liên quan đến SINR và link adaptation.

1	classdef nrUEAbstractPHY < nr5g.internal.nrUEPHY
2	    %nrUEAbstractPHY Implements abstract physical (PHY) layer for user equipment(UE)
3	    %   The class implements the abstraction specific aspects of UE PHY.
4	    %
5	    %   Note: This is an internal undocumented class and its API and/or
6	    %   functionality may change in subsequent releases.
7	
8	    %   Copyright 2022-2024 The MathWorks, Inc.
9	
10	    properties (Access = private)
11	        %L2SM Link-to-system-mapping (L2SM) context
12	        L2SM
13	    end
14	
15	    methods
16	        function obj = nrUEAbstractPHY(param, notificationFcn)
17	            %nrUEAbstractPHY Construct a UE PHY object
18	            %   OBJ = nrUEAbstractPHY(PARAM,NOTIFICATIONFCN) constructs a UE PHY object.
19	            %
20	            %   PARAM is a structure with the fields:
21	            %     TransmitPower       - UE Tx power in dBm
22	            %     NumTransmitAntennas - Number of Tx antennas on the UE
23	            %     NumReceiveAntennas  - Number of Rx antennas on the UE
24	            %     NoiseFigure         - Noise figure
25	            %     ReceiveGain         - Receiver gain at UE in dBi
26	            %
27	            %   NOTIFICATIONFCN - It is a handle of the node's processEvents
28	            %   method
29	
30	            obj = obj@nr5g.internal.nrUEPHY(param, notificationFcn); % Call base class constructor
31	
32	            % NR packet param
33	            obj.PacketStruct.Abstraction = true; % Abstracted PHY
34	            obj.PacketStruct.Metadata = struct('NCellID', [], 'RNTI', [], 'PrecodingMatrix', [], ...
35	                'NumSamples', [], 'Channel', obj.PacketStruct.Metadata.Channel);
36	        end
37	
38	        function addConnection(obj, connectionConfig)
39	            %addConnection Configures the UE PHY with connection information
40	            %   connectionConfig is a structure including the following
41	            %   fields:
42	            %       RNTI                     - Radio network temporary identifier
43	            %                                  specified within [1, 65522]. Refer
44	            %                                  table 7.1-1 in 3GPP TS 38.321 version 18.1.0
45	            %       NCellID                  - Physical cell ID. values: 0 to 1007 (TS 38.211, sec 7.4.2.1)
46	            %       DuplexMode               - "FDD" or "TDD"
47	            %       SubcarrierSpacing        - Subcarrier spacing
48	            %       NumResourceBlocks        - Number of resource blocks (RBs)
49	            %       NumHARQ                  - Number of HARQ processes on UE
50	            %       ChannelBandwidth         - DL or UL channel bandwidth in Hz
51	            %       DLCarrierFrequency       - DL carrier frequency
52	            %       ULCarrierFrequency       - UL carrier frequency
53	            %       CSIReportConfiguration   - CSI report configuration
54	
55	            addConnection@nr5g.internal.nrUEPHY(obj, connectionConfig);
56	            % Initialize L2SM to hold PDSCH, CSI-RS and inter-user interferer context
57	            obj.L2SM = nr5g.internal.L2SM.initialize(obj.CarrierConfig, connectionConfig.NumHARQ, 1);
58	            obj.L2SMCSI = nr5g.internal.L2SM.initialize(obj.CarrierConfig);
59	            obj.L2SMIUI = nr5g.internal.L2SM.initialize(obj.CarrierConfig);
60	        end
61	
62	        function data = puschData(obj, puschInfo, macPDU)
63	            % Return the MAC packet without any PHY processing
64	
65	            if isempty(macPDU)
66	                % MAC PDU not sent by MAC, which indicates retransmission. Get the MAC PDU
67	                % from the HARQ buffers
68	                data = obj.HARQBuffers{puschInfo.HARQID+1};
69	            else
70	                % New transmission. Buffer the transport block
71	                data = macPDU;
72	                obj.HARQBuffers{puschInfo.HARQID+1} = macPDU;
73	            end
74	        end
75	
76	        function data = srsData(~, ~)
77	            % Return empty as abstract PHY does not send any SRS waveform
78	
79	            data = [];
80	        end
81	
82	        function [macPDU, crcFlag, sinr] = decodePDSCH(obj, pdschInfo, pktStartTime, pktEndTime, carrierConfigInfo)
83	            % Return the decoded MAC PDU along with the crc result
84	
85	            % Read all the relevant packets (i.e either of interest or sent on same
86	            % carrier frequency) received during the PDSCH reception
87	            packetInfoList = packetList(obj.RxBuffer, pktStartTime, pktEndTime);
88	
89	            % Eliminate any PDSCH packets which are not sent on the overlapping resource
90	            % blocks as the PDSCH of interest. Also, separate out PDSCH of interest
91	            numPkts = numel(packetInfoList);
92	            interferingPackets = packetInfoList;
93	            prbSetPacket = pdschInfo.PDSCHConfig.PRBSet;
94	            numRBPacket = numel(prbSetPacket);
95	            packetOfInterest = [];
96	            intfPktCount=0;
97	            for pktIdx = 1:numPkts
98	                metadata = packetInfoList(pktIdx).Metadata;
99	                if (metadata.PacketType == obj.PXSCHPacketType) % Check for PDSCH
100	                    if (carrierConfigInfo.NCellID == metadata.NCellID) && ... % Check for PDSCH of interest
101	                            (pdschInfo.PDSCHConfig.RNTI == metadata.RNTI)
102	                        packetOfInterest = packetInfoList(pktIdx);
103	                    else
104	                        prbSetInterferer = metadata.PacketConfig.PRBSet;
105	                        isMatched = false;
106	                        numRBInterferer = numel(prbSetInterferer);
107	                        % Check for interfering packets
108	                        for i=1:numRBPacket
109	                            rbOfInterest = prbSetPacket(i);
110	                            for j=1:numRBInterferer
111	                                interferingRB = prbSetInterferer(j);
112	                                if interferingRB == rbOfInterest
113	                                    isMatched = true; % Packet is an interfering one
114	                                    intfPktCount = intfPktCount+1;
115	                                    interferingPackets(intfPktCount) = packetInfoList(pktIdx);
116	                                    break;
117	                                elseif interferingRB>rbOfInterest
118	                                    % The other values in the interfering
119	                                    % RB set are only going to be bigger
120	                                    % than current interfering RB
121	                                    break;
122	                                end
123	                            end
124	                            if isMatched
125	                                break;
126	                            end
127	                        end
128	                    end
129	                end
130	            end
131	            interferingPackets = interferingPackets(1:intfPktCount);
132	
133	            % Estimate channel for all for relevant packets i.e. packet of interest and
134	            % interferers (inter-cell interferers and inter-user interferers)
135	            [estChannelGrid, estChannelGridIntf] = estimateChannelGrid(obj, packetOfInterest, ...
136	                interferingPackets, carrierConfigInfo);
137	
138	            % Read MAC PDU
139	            macPDU = packetOfInterest.Data;
140	
141	            % Calculate crc result using l2sm
142	            [crcFlag, sinr] = l2smCRC(obj, packetOfInterest, interferingPackets, estChannelGrid, ...
143	                estChannelGridIntf, pdschInfo, carrierConfigInfo);
144	        end
145	
146	        function [dlRank, pmiSet, cqi, precodingMatrix, sinr] = decodeCSIRS(obj, csirsConfig, pktStartTime, pktEndTime, carrierConfigInfo)
147	            % Return CSI-RS measurement.
148	            %
149	            % ThangTQ23_128T128R_Rel19: For 128T, four Row-18 (32-port) CSI-RS
150	            % resources are spread across 2 slots (R0/R1 at slot+0, R2/R3 at
151	            % slot+1).  Each call to decodeCSIRS accumulates one resource into
152	            % obj.CSIRSChannelBuffer.  PMI selection (nrRISelect + LQM + CQI)
153	            % runs only when all 4 resources are buffered.  Before that, the
154	            % function returns dlRank = -1 (sentinel) so that csirsRx() defers
155	            % the MAC indication.
156	
157	            % ── Step 1: find packet + estimate channel ──────────────────────
158	            [csirsPacket, interferingPackets] = packetListIntfBuffer(obj, obj.CSIRSPacketType, ...
159	                pktStartTime, pktEndTime);
160	
161	            nVar = calculateThermalNoise(obj);
162	            obj.GNBReceivedPower = csirsPacket.Power;
163	
164	            rnti = obj.RNTI;
165	            packetOfInterest = [];
166	            for pktIdx = 1:numel(csirsPacket)
167	                if any(rnti == csirsPacket(pktIdx).Metadata.RNTI)
168	                    packetOfInterest = csirsPacket(pktIdx);
169	                    break;
170	                end
171	            end
172	
173	            [estChannelGrid, estChannelGridIntf] = estimateChannelGrid(obj, packetOfInterest, ...
174	                interferingPackets, carrierConfigInfo);
175	
176	            intf = prepareLQMInputIntf(obj, obj.L2SMIUI, interferingPackets, estChannelGridIntf, ...
177	                carrierConfigInfo, nVar);
178	
179	            % ── Step 2: 128T accumulation path (Row 18 = 32-port CDM8 resource)
180	            % ThangTQ23_128T128R_Rel19: aggregate H across all resources
181	            % before running PMI.  numResources128T = 4 for 128T.
182	            numResources128T = 4;
183	            if csirsConfig.RowNumber == 18 && csirsConfig.NumCSIRSPorts == 32
184	                % ThangTQ23_128T128R_Rel19: In abstract PHY, nrPerfectChannelEstimate
185	                % already returns the full 128-port channel [K x L x nRx x 128]
186	                % for every CSI-RS resource event (gNB has 128 antennas).
187	                % DO NOT concatenate along dim-4; instead accumulate as a running
188	                % average across all 4 resources for improved noise averaging.
189	                buf = obj.CSIRSChannelBuffer;
190	                if buf.count == 0
191	                    buf.H = estChannelGrid;
192	                else
193	                    buf.H = (buf.H * buf.count + estChannelGrid) / (buf.count + 1);
194	                end
195	                buf.count  = buf.count + 1;
196	                buf.nVar   = (buf.nVar * (buf.count - 1) + nVar) / buf.count;
197	                buf.intf   = intf;        % approximation: use latest resource intf
198	                buf.carrier = carrierConfigInfo;
199	                obj.CSIRSChannelBuffer = buf;
200	
201	                if buf.count < numResources128T
202	                    % Not enough resources yet — return sentinel to defer report
203	                    dlRank = -1;
204	                    pmiSet = struct('i1', NaN(1,3), 'i2', NaN);
205	                    cqi = 0;  precodingMatrix = [];  sinr = -Inf;
206	                    return;
207	                end
208	
209	                % All resources received: use averaged 128-port H for PMI
210	                estChannelGrid  = buf.H;         % [K x L x nRx x 128]
211	                nVar            = buf.nVar;
212	                intf            = buf.intf;
213	                carrierConfigInfo = buf.carrier;
214	
215	                % Reset buffer for next period
216	                obj.CSIRSChannelBuffer = struct('H', [], 'count', 0, 'nVar', 0, ...
217	                                                'intf', [], 'carrier', []);
218	
219	                % Build eTypeII-r19 report config for 128 ports.
220	                % Phase 3 will inject this via connectUE; here we derive a
221	                % default so that Phase 2 is immediately testable.
222	                reportCfg128 = obj.CSIReportConfig;
223	                if ~strcmpi(reportCfg128.CodebookType, 'eTypeII-r19')
224	                    % Build 128T eTypeII-r19 config as a struct so that all
225	                    % mandatory fields (NStartBWP, NSizeBWP, CQITable, etc.)
226	                    % are carried over from the validated base config.
227	                    reportCfg128.CodebookType                   = 'eTypeII-r19';
228	                    reportCfg128.PanelDimensions                = [1 16 4]; % N1=16,N2=4 → 128 ports
229	                    reportCfg128.NumberOfBeams                  = 2;        % L=2 (PC1, tractable)
230	                    reportCfg128.ParameterCombination           = 1;
231	                    reportCfg128.SubbandAmplitude               = false;    % wideband
232	                    % eTypeII-r19 specific fields not present in TypeI struct
233	                    reportCfg128.NumberOfPMISubbandsPerCQISubband = 1;
234	                end
235	
236	                % Run PMI selection on aggregated 128-port channel
237	                [dlRank, pmiSet, pmiInfo] = nr5g.internal.nrRISelect(carrierConfigInfo, ...
238	                    csirsConfig, reportCfg128, estChannelGrid, nVar, 'MaxSE');
239	            else
240	                % ── Standard path (≤32T, single resource) ──────────────────
241	                if csirsConfig.NumCSIRSPorts > 1
242	                    [dlRank, pmiSet, pmiInfo] = nr5g.internal.nrRISelect(carrierConfigInfo, ...
243	                        csirsConfig, obj.CSIReportConfig, estChannelGrid, nVar, 'MaxSE');
244	                else
245	                    dlRank = 1;
246	                    pmiSet = struct('i1', [1 1 1], 'i2', 1);
247	                    pmiInfo.W = 1;
248	                end
249	            end
250	
251	            % ── Step 3: CQI via LQM ─────────────────────────────────────────
252	            blerThreshold = 0.1;
253	            overhead = 0;
254	            if obj.CSIReferenceResource.NumLayers ~= dlRank
255	                obj.CSIReferenceResource.NumLayers = dlRank;
256	            end
257	            precodingMatrix = pmiInfo.W;
258	
259	            % ThangTQ23_128T128R_Rel19 Phase 4/4b: pmiInfo.W may be 3-D
260	            % [numPorts×rank×numSubbands] when PMIMode='Subband'.
261	            numSBs = size(pmiInfo.W, 3); % 1 for wideband, >1 for subband
262	
263	            useCQISubband = numSBs > 1 && ...
264	                            isfield(obj.CSIReportConfig, 'CQIMode') && ...
265	                            strcmpi(obj.CSIReportConfig.CQIMode, 'Subband');
266	
267	            if useCQISubband
268	                % ── Phase 4b: per-subband CQI ──────────────────────────────
269	                % One L2SM call per subband using the subband's own precoder W.
270	                % Each call uses the FULL H (same carrier / csirsConfig) so no
271	                % dimension mismatch; only W varies per subband.
272	                % Result: cqi is [1 × numRBs] with per-RB values.
273	                cqiPerSB  = zeros(1, numSBs);
274	                sinrPerSB = zeros(1, numSBs);
275	                csRef = obj.CSIReferenceResource;  % value copy
276	                for sb = 1:numSBs
277	                    W_sb    = pmiInfo.W(:,:,sb).';   % [rank × numPorts]
278	                    l2sm_sb = obj.L2SMCSI;           % value copy — does not mutate obj.L2SMCSI
279	                    [l2sm_sb, sig_sb]  = nr5g.internal.L2SM.prepareLQMInput(l2sm_sb, ...
280	                        carrierConfigInfo, csirsConfig, estChannelGrid, nVar, W_sb);
281	                    [l2sm_sb, sinr_sb] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.linkQualityModel(l2sm_sb, sig_sb, intf);
282	                    [~, cqi_sb, cqiInfo_sb] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.cqiSelect(l2sm_sb, ...
283	                        carrierConfigInfo, csRef, overhead, sinr_sb, obj.CQITableValues, blerThreshold);
284	                    cqiPerSB(sb)  = max(cqi_sb, 1);
285	                    sinrPerSB(sb) = cqiInfo_sb.EffectiveSINR;
286	                end
287	                % Expand to per-RB: sbSizeRBs = NSizeGrid / numSBs
288	                sbSizeRBs = round(carrierConfigInfo.NSizeGrid / numSBs);
289	                cqi  = repelem(cqiPerSB, sbSizeRBs);  % [1 × numRBs]
290	                sinr = sinrPerSB(1);  % first-subband SINR for event logging
291	                % Update L2SMCSI state using subband-1 W (keeps the obj state consistent)
292	                W_lqm = pmiInfo.W(:,:,1).';
293	                [obj.L2SMCSI, ~] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.prepareLQMInput(obj.L2SMCSI, ...
294	                    carrierConfigInfo, csirsConfig, estChannelGrid, nVar, W_lqm);
295	            else
296	                % ── Wideband CQI (Phase 3 / fast path) ─────────────────────
297	                if numSBs > 1
298	                    W_lqm = pmiInfo.W(:,:,1).';  % subband PMI, wideband CQI: use sb-1 proxy
299	                else
300	                    W_lqm = pmiInfo.W.';          % true wideband W
301	                end
302	                [obj.L2SMCSI, sig] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.prepareLQMInput(obj.L2SMCSI, ...
303	                    carrierConfigInfo, csirsConfig, estChannelGrid, nVar, W_lqm);
304	                [obj.L2SMCSI, sinr] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.linkQualityModel(obj.L2SMCSI, sig, intf);
305	                [obj.L2SMCSI, cqi, cqiInfo] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.cqiSelect(obj.L2SMCSI, ...
306	                    carrierConfigInfo, obj.CSIReferenceResource, overhead, sinr, obj.CQITableValues, blerThreshold);
307	                cqi  = max([cqi, 1]);
308	                sinr = cqiInfo.EffectiveSINR;
309	            end
310	
311	            % ThangTQ23_128T128R_Rel19: Live CSI feedback log — print UE-1 and UE-2 only.
312	            % Shows RI / CQI (per subband or wideband) / PMI i1 / SINR / overhead estimate.
313	            if rnti <= 2
314	                absSlot  = carrierConfigInfo.NFrame * carrierConfigInfo.SlotsPerFrame + carrierConfigInfo.NSlot;
315	                ri_bits  = 2;    % ceil(log2(maxRank=4))
316	                pmi_bits = 20;   % approx eTypeII-r19 L=2: beam indices + wideband amplitudes
317	                if useCQISubband
318	                    oh_cqi_bits = 4 + 2*(numSBs - 1);  % wideband base + differential subbands
319	                    cqi_str     = ['[' strtrim(num2str(cqiPerSB, '%d ')) '] (' num2str(numSBs) 'sb)'];
320	                    sinr_str    = ['[' strtrim(num2str(sinrPerSB, '%.1f ')) '] dB'];
321	                else
322	                    oh_cqi_bits = 4;
323	                    cqi_str     = sprintf('%d (WB)', cqi);
324	                    sinr_str    = sprintf('%.1f dB', sinr);
325	                end
326	                oh_total  = ri_bits + oh_cqi_bits + pmi_bits;
327	                % Show only first 3 i1 components (spatial beam indices) — eTypeII-r19 i1 has 28+ elements
328	                i1_disp = pmiSet.i1(1 : min(3, numel(pmiSet.i1)));
329	                i1_str  = strtrim(num2str(i1_disp, '%d '));
330	                fprintf('[CSI@slot%4d] UE-%-2d  RI=%d  CQI=%s  SINR=%s  i1=[%s]  OH~%d bits\n', ...
331	                    absSlot, rnti, dlRank, cqi_str, sinr_str, i1_str, oh_total);
332	            end
333	        end
334	    end
335	
336	    methods (Access = private)
337	        function [crcFlag, sinr] = l2smCRC(obj, packetOfInterest, interferingPackets, estChannelGrid, ...
338	                estChannelGridsIntf, pdschInfo, carrierConfigInfo)
339	            % Return CRC flag (0 - success, 1 - failure)
340	
341	            % Noise Variance
342	            nVar = calculateThermalNoise(obj);
343	
344	            % Prepare LQM input for interferers
345	            intf = prepareLQMInputIntf(obj, obj.L2SMIUI, interferingPackets, estChannelGridsIntf, carrierConfigInfo, nVar);
346	
347	            % Prepare LQM input for packet of interest
348	            % Extract PDSCH Indices for the packet of interest
349	            [~, info] = nrPDSCHIndices(carrierConfigInfo, pdschInfo.PDSCHConfig);
350	            % Prepare HARQ context for the packet of interest
351	            harqInfo = struct('HARQProcessID', pdschInfo.HARQID, 'RedundancyVersion', pdschInfo.RV, ...
352	                'TransportBlockSize', pdschInfo.TBS*8, 'NewData', pdschInfo.NewData);
353	            obj.L2SM = nr5g.internal.L2SM.txHARQ(obj.L2SM, harqInfo, pdschInfo.TargetCodeRate, info.G);
354	            % Prepare Link Quality Model inputs for the packet of interest
355	            [obj.L2SM, sig] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.prepareLQMInput(obj.L2SM,carrierConfigInfo, ...
356	                packetOfInterest.Metadata.PacketConfig, estChannelGrid, nVar, packetOfInterest.Metadata.PrecodingMatrix);
357	
358	            % Link Quality Model (LQM) with signal of interest and interference
359	            [obj.L2SM,sinr] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.linkQualityModel(obj.L2SM,sig,intf);
360	
361	            % Link Performance Model
362	            [obj.L2SM, crcFlag, cqiInfo] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.linkPerformanceModel(obj.L2SM,harqInfo,pdschInfo.PDSCHConfig,sinr);
363	            sinr = cqiInfo.EffectiveSINR;
364	        end
365	    end
366	end

1	classdef nrComponentCarrierContext < handle
2	    %nrComponentCarrierContext Carrier-specific information of a UE at gNB scheduler
3	    %
4	    %   This class reprsents the information which scheduler maintains for a
5	    %   connected UE on a particular carrier.
6	    %
7	    %   Note: This is an internal undocumented class and its API and/or
8	    %   functionality may change in subsequent releases.
9	
10	    %   Copyright 2024 The MathWorks, Inc.
11	
12	    properties(SetAccess=private)
13	        %NumTransmitAntennas Number of transmit antennas at the UE
14	        NumTransmitAntennas
15	
16	        %NumReceiveAntennas Number of receive antennas at the UE
17	        NumReceiveAntennas
18	
19	        %CSIRSConfiguration CSI-RS configuration of the UE
20	        CSIRSConfiguration
21	
22	        %SRSConfiguration SRS configuration of the UE
23	        SRSConfiguration
24	
25	        %CSIMeasurementDL Wideband DL channel measurement for the UE node based on CSI-RS and SRS
26	        % It is specified as a structure with these fields.
27	        %
28	        % CSIRS — A structure with these fields:
29	        %      RI — Rank indicator
30	        %      PMISet — Precoder matrix indicator (PMI) set
31	        %      CQI — Channel quality indicator
32	        %      W — Precoding matrix
33	        %
34	        % SRS — A structure with these fields:
35	        %       RI — Rank indicator
36	        %       W — Precoding matrix
37	        %       MCSIndex — Modulation and coding scheme index
38	        CSIMeasurementDL
39	
40	        %CSIMeasurementUL UL CSI measurements based on SRS configured
41	        % It is a structure with the fields: 'RI', 'TPMI', 'MCSIndex'
42	        CSIMeasurementUL
43	    end
44	
45	    properties(Hidden)
46	        %RetransmissionContextDL Information about downlink retransmission requirements
47	        % 1-by-P matrix where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. It stores the
48	        % information of HARQ processes for which the reception failed at gNB. This
49	        % information is used for assigning downlink assignments for
50	        % retransmissions. 1 indicates that the reception has failed for this
51	        % particular HARQ process ID governed by the column index. 0 indicates that
52	        % the HARQ process does not have any retransmission requirement.
53	        RetransmissionContextDL
54	
55	        %RetransmissionContextUL Information about uplink retransmission requirements
56	        % 1-by-P matrix where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. It stores the
57	        % information of HARQ processes for which the reception failed at gNB. This
58	        % information is used for assigning uplink grants for retransmissions. 1
59	        % indicates that the reception has failed for this particular HARQ process
60	        % ID governed by the column index. 0 indicates that the HARQ process does
61	        % not have any retransmission requirement.
62	        RetransmissionContextUL
63	
64	        %HarqProcessesDL Downlink HARQ processes context
65	        % 1-by-P structure array where 'P' is the number of DL HARQ
66	        % processes. Value at index 'i' contains the HARQ context of
67	        % process with HARQ ID 'i-1'
68	        HarqProcessesDL
69	
70	        %HarqProcessesUL Uplink HARQ processes context
71	        % 1-by-P structure array where 'P' is the number of UL HARQ
72	        % processes. Value at index 'i' contains the HARQ context of
73	        % process with HARQ ID 'i-1'
74	        HarqProcessesUL
75	
76	        %TBSizeDL Stores the size of transport block sent for DL HARQ processes
77	        % 1-by-P matrix where P is number of HARQ process. Value at index j stores
78	        % size of transport block sent for HARQ process index 'j'. Value is
79	        % 0 if DL HARQ process is free
80	        TBSizeDL
81	
82	        %TBSizeUL Stores the size of transport block to be received for UL HARQ processes
83	        % 1-by-P matrix where P is number of HARQ process. Value at index j stores
84	        % size of transport block to be received from UE for HARQ process index
85	        % 'j'. Value is 0, if no UL packet is expected for HARQ process of the UE
86	        TBSizeUL
87	
88	        %HarqNDIDL Last sent NDI value for the DL HARQ processes
89	        % 1-by-P logical array where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. Values at
90	        % index j stores the last sent NDI for the DL HARQ process index 'j'
91	        HarqNDIDL
92	
93	        %HarqNDIUL Last sent NDI value for the UL HARQ processes
94	        % 1-by-P logical array where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. Values at
95	        % index j stores the last sent NDI for the UL HARQ process index 'j'
96	        HarqNDIUL
97	
98	        %HarqStatusDL Status (free or busy) of each downlink HARQ process
99	        % 1-by-P cell array where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. A
100	        % non-empty value at index j indicates that HARQ process is busy with value
101	        % being the downlink assignment for the UE with HARQ index 'j'. Empty value
102	        % indicates that the HARQ process is free.
103	        HarqStatusDL
104	
105	        %HarqStatusUL Status (free or busy) of each uplink HARQ process
106	        % 1-by-P cell array where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. A non-empty
107	        % value at index j indicates that HARQ process is busy with value being the
108	        % uplink grant for the UE with HARQ index 'j'. Empty value indicates that
109	        % the HARQ process is free.
110	        HarqStatusUL
111	
112	        %IsBusyHARQDL Status (free or busy) of each downlink HARQ process of the UE
113	        % 1-by-P matrix where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. 1 indicates busy
114	        % HARQ process and 0 indicates that the HARQ process is free.
115	        IsBusyHARQDL
116	
117	        %IsBusyHARQUL Status (free or busy) of each uplink HARQ process
118	        % 1-by-P matrix where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. 1 indicates busy
119	        % HARQ process and 0 indicates that the HARQ process is free.
120	        IsBusyHARQUL
121	
122	        %RBGSize Size of a resource block group (RBG) in terms of number of RBs
123	        RBGSize
124	
125	        %NumRBGs Number of RBGs in the bandwidth
126	        NumRBGs
127	
128	        %MCSOffset Specifies the current MCS offset applied to the PXSCH transmissions.
129	        % It is a 1-by-2 matrix with corresponding MCS offsets for
130	        %DL/UL transmissions.
131	        MCSOffset
132	
133	        %XOverheadPDSCH Additional overheads in PDSCH transmission
134	        XOverheadPDSCH = 0;
135	
136	        %PrecodingGranularity PDSCH precoding granularity in terms of physical resource blocks (PRBs)
137	        PrecodingGranularity
138	    end
139	
140	    methods(Hidden)
141	        function obj = nrComponentCarrierContext(param, cellConfig, schedulerConfig)
142	            %nrComponentCarrierContext Initialize carrier context
143	            %
144	            % PARAM is a structure including the following fields:
145	            % NumTransmitAntennas          - Number of UE Tx antennas
146	            % NumReceiveAntennas           - Number of UE Rx antennas
147	            % CSIRSConfiguration           - CSI-RS configuration of the UE corresponding to PrimaryCarrierIndex
148	            % SRSConfiguration             - SRS configuration of the UE corresponding to PrimaryCarrierIndex
149	            % NumHARQ                      - Number of HARQ processes
150	            % RBGSizeConfig                - RBG configuration to derive number of RBs in an RBG
151	            % InitialCQIDL                 - Initial DL CQI
152	            % InitialMCSIndexUL            - Initial UL MCS
153	            % InitialMCSOffsetDL           - Initial MCS offset (link adaptation) for DL direction
154	            % InitialMCSOffsetUL           - Initial MCS offset (link adaptation) for UL direction
155	
156	            % CELLCONFIG is cell configuration.
157	            % SCHEDULERCONFIG is scheduler configuration.
158	
159	            if ~isempty(param)
160	                % Initialize the properties
161	                inputParam = ["NumTransmitAntennas", "NumReceiveAntennas", "CSIRSConfiguration", "SRSConfiguration"];
162	                for idx=1:numel(inputParam)
163	                    obj.(inputParam(idx)) = param.(inputParam(idx));
164	                end
165	                numHARQ = param.NumHARQ;
166	                % Create retransmission context
167	                obj.HarqStatusDL = cell(1, numHARQ);
168	                obj.HarqStatusUL = cell(1, numHARQ);
169	                obj.RetransmissionContextDL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
170	                obj.RetransmissionContextUL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
171	                obj.IsBusyHARQDL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
172	                obj.IsBusyHARQUL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
173	                obj.HarqNDIDL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
174	                obj.HarqNDIUL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
175	                obj.TBSizeDL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
176	                obj.TBSizeUL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
177	                ncw = 1; % Only single codeword
178	                % Create HARQ processes context array
179	                obj.HarqProcessesDL = nr5g.internal.nrNewHARQProcesses(numHARQ, schedulerConfig.RVSequence, ncw);
180	                obj.HarqProcessesUL = nr5g.internal.nrNewHARQProcesses(numHARQ, schedulerConfig.RVSequence, ncw);
181	
182	                % RBGSize configuration as 1 (configuration-1 RBG table) or 2
183	                % (configuration-2 RBG table) as defined in 3GPP TS 38.214 Section
184	                % 5.1.2.2.1. It defines the number of RBs in an RBG.
185	                nominalRBGSizePerBW = nr5g.internal.MACConstants.NominalRBGSizePerBW;
186	                rbgSizeIndex = min(find(cellConfig.NumResourceBlocks <= nominalRBGSizePerBW(:, 1), 1));
187	                if param.RBGSizeConfig == 1
188	                    obj.RBGSize = nominalRBGSizePerBW(rbgSizeIndex, 2);
189	                else % RBGSizeConfig is 2
190	                    obj.RBGSize = nominalRBGSizePerBW(rbgSizeIndex, 3);
191	                end
192	                obj.NumRBGs = ceil(cellConfig.NumResourceBlocks/obj.RBGSize);
193	
194	                % Set PDSCH overhead
195	                if ~isempty(param.CSIRSConfiguration)
196	                    obj.XOverheadPDSCH = 18;
197	                    % ThangTQ23_128T128R_Rel19: for array CSIRSConfiguration (128T uses
198	                    % 4×32-port resources), sum all port counts to get total antenna ports.
199	                    numCSIRSPorts = sum([obj.CSIRSConfiguration.NumCSIRSPorts]);
200	                else
201	                    numCSIRSPorts = cellConfig.NumTransmitAntennas;
202	                end
203	
204	                % ThangTQ23_128T128R_Rel19 Phase 4/4b: PrecodingGranularity depends on
205	                % whether subband PMI is active. Use CSIReportConfiguration.PMIMode
206	                % (set by nrNodeValidation from the pmiCQIMode flag) so wideband and
207	                % subband modes are both supported without hardcoding.
208	                useSubbandPRG = isfield(param, 'CSIReportConfiguration') && ...
209	                                ~isempty(param.CSIReportConfiguration) && ...
210	                                isfield(param.CSIReportConfiguration, 'PMIMode') && ...
211	                                strcmpi(param.CSIReportConfiguration.PMIMode, 'Subband');
212	                if useSubbandPRG
213	                    obj.PrecodingGranularity = 4; % PMI subband size = 4 RBs (TS 38.214 Table 5.2.1.4-2, NRB∈[24,72])
214	                else
215	                    obj.PrecodingGranularity = cellConfig.NumResourceBlocks; % wideband: 1 PRG = full BW
216	                end
217	                % CSI measurements initialization (DL and UL)
218	                initialRank = 1; % Initial ranks for UEs
219	                wp = ones(numCSIRSPorts, 1)./sqrt(numCSIRSPorts);
220	                csirs = struct('RI', initialRank, 'PMISet', struct('i1',[1 1 1]), ...
221	                    'CQI', param.InitialCQIDL, 'W', wp);
222	                obj.CSIMeasurementDL = struct('CSIRS', csirs, 'SRS', []);
223	                obj.CSIMeasurementUL = struct('RI', initialRank, 'TPMI', 0, 'MCSIndex', param.InitialMCSIndexUL);
224	
225	                % Initialize link adaptation MCS offset values for the carrier. The
226	                % first column contains MCS offset in DL direction and second column
227	                % contains MCS offset in UL direction.
228	                obj.MCSOffset = [0 0];
229	                if ~isempty(schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigDL)
230	                    obj.MCSOffset(1) = param.InitialMCSOffsetDL;
231	                end
232	                if ~isempty(schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigUL)
233	                    obj.MCSOffset(2) = param.InitialMCSOffsetUL;
234	                end
235	            end
236	        end
237	
238	        function updateChannelQualityDL(obj, channelQualityInfo, schedulerConfig)
239	            %updateChannelQualityDL Update downlink channel quality information for a UE
240	            %   UPDATECHANNELQUALITYDL(OBJ, CHANNELQUALITYINFO) updates
241	            %   downlink (DL) channel quality information for a UE.
242	            %   CHANNELQUALITYINFO is a structure with these fields: RI, PMISet, W, CQI.
243	            %   SCHEDULERCONFIG is a structure containing field 'LinkAdaptationConfigDL'.
244	            %       LinkAdaptationConfigDL - Link adaptation (LA) configuration structure for downlink transmissions.
245	            %                          The structure contains these fields: InitialOffset, StepUp and StepDown
246	
247	            if schedulerConfig.CSIMeasurementSignalDLType && isfield(channelQualityInfo, 'SRSBasedDLMeasurements')
248	                % Update CSI measurement based on SRS
249	                obj.CSIMeasurementDL.SRS = channelQualityInfo.SRSBasedDLMeasurements;
250	            else
251	                % Update CSI measurement based on CSI-RS
252	                obj.CSIMeasurementDL.CSIRS.CQI = channelQualityInfo.CQI;
253	                obj.CSIMeasurementDL.CSIRS.RI = channelQualityInfo.RankIndicator;
254	                obj.CSIMeasurementDL.CSIRS.PMISet.i1 = channelQualityInfo.PMISet.i1;
255	                obj.CSIMeasurementDL.CSIRS.W = channelQualityInfo.W;
256	            end
257	            if ~isempty(schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigDL)
258	                % Reset link adaptation MCS offset value to initial offset
259	                obj.MCSOffset(1) = schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigDL.InitialOffset;
260	            end
261	        end
262	
263	        function updateChannelQualityUL(obj, channelQualityInfo, schedulerConfig)
264	            %updateChannelQualityUL Update uplink channel quality information for a UE
265	            %   UPDATECHANNELQUALITYUL(OBJ, CHANNELQUALITYINFO) updates
266	            %   uplink (UL) channel quality information for a UE.
267	            %   CHANNELQUALITYINFO is a structure with these fields: RI, TPMI, MCSIndex.
268	            %   SCHEDULERCONFIG is a structure containing field 'LinkAdaptationConfigUL'.
269	            %       LinkAdaptationConfigUL - Link adaptation (LA) configuration structure for uplink transmissions.
270	            %                          The structure contains these fields: InitialOffset, StepUp and StepDow
271	
272	            % Update CSI measurement
273	            obj.CSIMeasurementUL.MCSIndex = channelQualityInfo.MCSIndex;
274	            obj.CSIMeasurementUL.RI = channelQualityInfo.RankIndicator;
275	            obj.CSIMeasurementUL.TPMI = channelQualityInfo.TPMI;
276	            if ~isempty(schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigUL)
277	                % Reset link adaptation MCS offset value to initial offset
278	                obj.MCSOffset(2) = schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigUL.InitialOffset;
279	            end
280	        end
281	
282	        function updateSRSPeriod(obj, srsPeriod)
283	            %updateSRSPeriod Update the SRS periodicity of the UE for the specified carrier
284	
285	            obj.SRSConfiguration.SRSPeriod = srsPeriod;
286	        end
287	    end
288	end

file.content:
classdef nrComponentCarrierContext < handle
    %nrComponentCarrierContext Carrier-specific information of a UE at gNB scheduler
    %
    %   This class reprsents the information which scheduler maintains for a
    %   connected UE on a particular carrier.
    %
    %   Note: This is an internal undocumented class and its API and/or
    %   functionality may change in subsequent releases.

    %   Copyright 2024 The MathWorks, Inc.

    properties(SetAccess=private)
        %NumTransmitAntennas Number of transmit antennas at the UE
        NumTransmitAntennas

        %NumReceiveAntennas Number of receive antennas at the UE
        NumReceiveAntennas

        %CSIRSConfiguration CSI-RS configuration of the UE
        CSIRSConfiguration

        %SRSConfiguration SRS configuration of the UE
        SRSConfiguration

        %CSIMeasurementDL Wideband DL channel measurement for the UE node based on CSI-RS and SRS
        % It is specified as a structure with these fields.
        %
        % CSIRS — A structure with these fields:
        %      RI — Rank indicator
        %      PMISet — Precoder matrix indicator (PMI) set
        %      CQI — Channel quality indicator
        %      W — Precoding matrix
        %
        % SRS — A structure with these fields:
        %       RI — Rank indicator
        %       W — Precoding matrix
        %       MCSIndex — Modulation and coding scheme index
        CSIMeasurementDL

        %CSIMeasurementUL UL CSI measurements based on SRS configured
        % It is a structure with the fields: 'RI', 'TPMI', 'MCSIndex'
        CSIMeasurementUL
    end

    properties(Hidden)
        %RetransmissionContextDL Information about downlink retransmission requirements
        % 1-by-P matrix where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. It stores the
        % information of HARQ processes for which the reception failed at gNB. This
        % information is used for assigning downlink assignments for
        % retransmissions. 1 indicates that the reception has failed for this
        % particular HARQ process ID governed by the column index. 0 indicates that
        % the HARQ process does not have any retransmission requirement.
        RetransmissionContextDL

        %RetransmissionContextUL Information about uplink retransmission requirements
        % 1-by-P matrix where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. It stores the
        % information of HARQ processes for which the reception failed at gNB. This
        % information is used for assigning uplink grants for retransmissions. 1
        % indicates that the reception has failed for this particular HARQ process
        % ID governed by the column index. 0 indicates that the HARQ process does
        % not have any retransmission requirement.
        RetransmissionContextUL

        %HarqProcessesDL Downlink HARQ processes context
        % 1-by-P structure array where 'P' is the number of DL HARQ
        % processes. Value at index 'i' contains the HARQ context of
        % process with HARQ ID 'i-1'
        HarqProcessesDL

        %HarqProcessesUL Uplink HARQ processes context
        % 1-by-P structure array where 'P' is the number of UL HARQ
        % processes. Value at index 'i' contains the HARQ context of
        % process with HARQ ID 'i-1'
        HarqProcessesUL

        %TBSizeDL Stores the size of transport block sent for DL HARQ processes
        % 1-by-P matrix where P is number of HARQ process. Value at index j stores
        % size of transport block sent for HARQ process index 'j'. Value is
        % 0 if DL HARQ process is free
        TBSizeDL

        %TBSizeUL Stores the size of transport block to be received for UL HARQ processes
        % 1-by-P matrix where P is number of HARQ process. Value at index j stores
        % size of transport block to be received from UE for HARQ process index
        % 'j'. Value is 0, if no UL packet is expected for HARQ process of the UE
        TBSizeUL

        %HarqNDIDL Last sent NDI value for the DL HARQ processes
        % 1-by-P logical array where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. Values at
        % index j stores the last sent NDI for the DL HARQ process index 'j'
        HarqNDIDL

        %HarqNDIUL Last sent NDI value for the UL HARQ processes
        % 1-by-P logical array where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. Values at
        % index j stores the last sent NDI for the UL HARQ process index 'j'
        HarqNDIUL

        %HarqStatusDL Status (free or busy) of each downlink HARQ process
        % 1-by-P cell array where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. A
        % non-empty value at index j indicates that HARQ process is busy with value
        % being the downlink assignment for the UE with HARQ index 'j'. Empty value
        % indicates that the HARQ process is free.
        HarqStatusDL

        %HarqStatusUL Status (free or busy) of each uplink HARQ process
        % 1-by-P cell array where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. A non-empty
        % value at index j indicates that HARQ process is busy with value being the
        % uplink grant for the UE with HARQ index 'j'. Empty value indicates that
        % the HARQ process is free.
        HarqStatusUL

        %IsBusyHARQDL Status (free or busy) of each downlink HARQ process of the UE
        % 1-by-P matrix where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. 1 indicates busy
        % HARQ process and 0 indicates that the HARQ process is free.
        IsBusyHARQDL

        %IsBusyHARQUL Status (free or busy) of each uplink HARQ process
        % 1-by-P matrix where 'P' is the number of HARQ processes. 1 indicates busy
        % HARQ process and 0 indicates that the HARQ process is free.
        IsBusyHARQUL

        %RBGSize Size of a resource block group (RBG) in terms of number of RBs
        RBGSize

        %NumRBGs Number of RBGs in the bandwidth
        NumRBGs

        %MCSOffset Specifies the current MCS offset applied to the PXSCH transmissions.
        % It is a 1-by-2 matrix with corresponding MCS offsets for
        %DL/UL transmissions.
        MCSOffset

        %XOverheadPDSCH Additional overheads in PDSCH transmission
        XOverheadPDSCH = 0;

        %PrecodingGranularity PDSCH precoding granularity in terms of physical resource blocks (PRBs)
        PrecodingGranularity
    end

    methods(Hidden)
        function obj = nrComponentCarrierContext(param, cellConfig, schedulerConfig)
            %nrComponentCarrierContext Initialize carrier context
            %
            % PARAM is a structure including the following fields:
            % NumTransmitAntennas          - Number of UE Tx antennas
            % NumReceiveAntennas           - Number of UE Rx antennas
            % CSIRSConfiguration           - CSI-RS configuration of the UE corresponding to PrimaryCarrierIndex
            % SRSConfiguration             - SRS configuration of the UE corresponding to PrimaryCarrierIndex
            % NumHARQ                      - Number of HARQ processes
            % RBGSizeConfig                - RBG configuration to derive number of RBs in an RBG
            % InitialCQIDL                 - Initial DL CQI
            % InitialMCSIndexUL            - Initial UL MCS
            % InitialMCSOffsetDL           - Initial MCS offset (link adaptation) for DL direction
            % InitialMCSOffsetUL           - Initial MCS offset (link adaptation) for UL direction

            % CELLCONFIG is cell configuration.
            % SCHEDULERCONFIG is scheduler configuration.

            if ~isempty(param)
                % Initialize the properties
                inputParam = ["NumTransmitAntennas", "NumReceiveAntennas", "CSIRSConfiguration", "SRSConfiguration"];
                for idx=1:numel(inputParam)
                    obj.(inputParam(idx)) = param.(inputParam(idx));
                end
                numHARQ = param.NumHARQ;
                % Create retransmission context
                obj.HarqStatusDL = cell(1, numHARQ);
                obj.HarqStatusUL = cell(1, numHARQ);
                obj.RetransmissionContextDL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
                obj.RetransmissionContextUL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
                obj.IsBusyHARQDL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
                obj.IsBusyHARQUL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
                obj.HarqNDIDL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
                obj.HarqNDIUL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
                obj.TBSizeDL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
                obj.TBSizeUL = zeros(1, numHARQ);
                ncw = 1; % Only single codeword
                % Create HARQ processes context array
                obj.HarqProcessesDL = nr5g.internal.nrNewHARQProcesses(numHARQ, schedulerConfig.RVSequence, ncw);
                obj.HarqProcessesUL = nr5g.internal.nrNewHARQProcesses(numHARQ, schedulerConfig.RVSequence, ncw);

                % RBGSize configuration as 1 (configuration-1 RBG table) or 2
                % (configuration-2 RBG table) as defined in 3GPP TS 38.214 Section
                % 5.1.2.2.1. It defines the number of RBs in an RBG.
                nominalRBGSizePerBW = nr5g.internal.MACConstants.NominalRBGSizePerBW;
                rbgSizeIndex = min(find(cellConfig.NumResourceBlocks <= nominalRBGSizePerBW(:, 1), 1));
                if param.RBGSizeConfig == 1
                    obj.RBGSize = nominalRBGSizePerBW(rbgSizeIndex, 2);
                else % RBGSizeConfig is 2
                    obj.RBGSize = nominalRBGSizePerBW(rbgSizeIndex, 3);
                end
                obj.NumRBGs = ceil(cellConfig.NumResourceBlocks/obj.RBGSize);

                % Set PDSCH overhead
                if ~isempty(param.CSIRSConfiguration)
                    obj.XOverheadPDSCH = 18;
                    % ThangTQ23_128T128R_Rel19: for array CSIRSConfiguration (128T uses
                    % 4×32-port resources), sum all port counts to get total antenna ports.
                    numCSIRSPorts = sum([obj.CSIRSConfiguration.NumCSIRSPorts]);
                else
                    numCSIRSPorts = cellConfig.NumTransmitAntennas;
                end

                % ThangTQ23_128T128R_Rel19 Phase 4/4b: PrecodingGranularity depends on
                % whether subband PMI is active. Use CSIReportConfiguration.PMIMode
                % (set by nrNodeValidation from the pmiCQIMode flag) so wideband and
                % subband modes are both supported without hardcoding.
                useSubbandPRG = isfield(param, 'CSIReportConfiguration') && ...
                                ~isempty(param.CSIReportConfiguration) && ...
                                isfield(param.CSIReportConfiguration, 'PMIMode') && ...
                                strcmpi(param.CSIReportConfiguration.PMIMode, 'Subband');
                if useSubbandPRG
                    obj.PrecodingGranularity = 4; % PMI subband size = 4 RBs (TS 38.214 Table 5.2.1.4-2, NRB∈[24,72])
                else
                    obj.PrecodingGranularity = cellConfig.NumResourceBlocks; % wideband: 1 PRG = full BW
                end
                % CSI measurements initialization (DL and UL)
                initialRank = 1; % Initial ranks for UEs
                wp = ones(numCSIRSPorts, 1)./sqrt(numCSIRSPorts);
                csirs = struct('RI', initialRank, 'PMISet', struct('i1',[1 1 1]), ...
                    'CQI', param.InitialCQIDL, 'W', wp);
                obj.CSIMeasurementDL = struct('CSIRS', csirs, 'SRS', []);
                obj.CSIMeasurementUL = struct('RI', initialRank, 'TPMI', 0, 'MCSIndex', param.InitialMCSIndexUL);

                % Initialize link adaptation MCS offset values for the carrier. The
                % first column contains MCS offset in DL direction and second column
                % contains MCS offset in UL direction.
                obj.MCSOffset = [0 0];
                if ~isempty(schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigDL)
                    obj.MCSOffset(1) = param.InitialMCSOffsetDL;
                end
                if ~isempty(schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigUL)
                    obj.MCSOffset(2) = param.InitialMCSOffsetUL;
                end
            end
        end

        function updateChannelQualityDL(obj, channelQualityInfo, schedulerConfig)
            %updateChannelQualityDL Update downlink channel quality information for a UE
            %   UPDATECHANNELQUALITYDL(OBJ, CHANNELQUALITYINFO) updates
            %   downlink (DL) channel quality information for a UE.
            %   CHANNELQUALITYINFO is a structure with these fields: RI, PMISet, W, CQI.
            %   SCHEDULERCONFIG is a structure containing field 'LinkAdaptationConfigDL'.
            %       LinkAdaptationConfigDL - Link adaptation (LA) configuration structure for downlink transmissions.
            %                          The structure contains these fields: InitialOffset, StepUp and StepDown

            if schedulerConfig.CSIMeasurementSignalDLType && isfield(channelQualityInfo, 'SRSBasedDLMeasurements')
                % Update CSI measurement based on SRS
                obj.CSIMeasurementDL.SRS = channelQualityInfo.SRSBasedDLMeasurements;
            else
                % Update CSI measurement based on CSI-RS
                obj.CSIMeasurementDL.CSIRS.CQI = channelQualityInfo.CQI;
                obj.CSIMeasurementDL.CSIRS.RI = channelQualityInfo.RankIndicator;
                obj.CSIMeasurementDL.CSIRS.PMISet.i1 = channelQualityInfo.PMISet.i1;
                obj.CSIMeasurementDL.CSIRS.W = channelQualityInfo.W;
            end
            if ~isempty(schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigDL)
                % Reset link adaptation MCS offset value to initial offset
                obj.MCSOffset(1) = schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigDL.InitialOffset;
            end
        end

        function updateChannelQualityUL(obj, channelQualityInfo, schedulerConfig)
            %updateChannelQualityUL Update uplink channel quality information for a UE
            %   UPDATECHANNELQUALITYUL(OBJ, CHANNELQUALITYINFO) updates
            %   uplink (UL) channel quality information for a UE.
            %   CHANNELQUALITYINFO is a structure with these fields: RI, TPMI, MCSIndex.
            %   SCHEDULERCONFIG is a structure containing field 'LinkAdaptationConfigUL'.
            %       LinkAdaptationConfigUL - Link adaptation (LA) configuration structure for uplink transmissions.
            %                          The structure contains these fields: InitialOffset, StepUp and StepDow

            % Update CSI measurement
            obj.CSIMeasurementUL.MCSIndex = channelQualityInfo.MCSIndex;
            obj.CSIMeasurementUL.RI = channelQualityInfo.RankIndicator;
            obj.CSIMeasurementUL.TPMI = channelQualityInfo.TPMI;
            if ~isempty(schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigUL)
                % Reset link adaptation MCS offset value to initial offset
                obj.MCSOffset(2) = schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigUL.InitialOffset;
            end
        end

        function updateSRSPeriod(obj, srsPeriod)
            %updateSRSPeriod Update the SRS periodicity of the UE for the specified carrier

            obj.SRSConfiguration.SRSPeriod = srsPeriod;
        end
    end
end

content.thinking:
Now I can see clearly the issues. Let me analyze the log and code:

**Key observations from the log:**

1. **BLER ~60% for ALL UEs** - This is critically abnormal. Target BLER should be ~10%.

2. **SINR identical across all 6 subbands** - e.g., `SINR=[31.9 31.9 31.9 31.9 31.9 31.9] dB`. With a frequency-selective CDL-B channel, subbands should have different SINR values.

3. **CQI identical across subbands** - Consistent with uniform SINR.


4. **MCS min=0 for all UEs** - Frequent HARQ retransmissions at the lowest MCS level.

5. **MCS avg ~25-26 with BLER ~60%** - The link adaptation algorithm isn't reducing MCS despite the extremely high block error rate.

I found a critical bug in the channel quality update function: the MCS offset gets reset to its initial value every time a new CSI report arrives, which completely undermines the link adaptation mechanism's ability to adjust modulation and coding based on channel conditions.

The outer-loop link adaptation is supposed to accumulate negative offsets when BLER is high to force lower MCS values, but if that offset resets every 5 milliseconds when CSI updates come in, it never gets a chance to build up the correction needed. If the initial offset is zero, then resetting it means the algorithm starts from scratch each time, explaining why BLER stays persistently high despite the obvious need to reduce MCS.

There's also an issue with how subband SINR values are being logged—they're showing identical values across all subbands, which suggests the effective SINR calculation isn't properly differentiating between frequency resources. So the abstract PHY model computes an effective SINR averaged across the entire carrier rather than per-RB, which explains why all subbands report identical SINR values even though the CQI itself varies by subband. This isn't necessarily a bug—it's just how the L2SM abstraction works at the carrier level rather than granular per-resource-block resolution. E-2 has CQI=12 vs 15 at other times, and slot 950: UE-2 has CQI=9), so the subband CQI variation comes from different W_sb precoders, but the SINR printed is from `cqiInfo_sb.EffectiveSINR` which may use the same full-band averaging in L2SM.

Actually looking more carefully - in the log, the SINR values are the same across all subbands within each report. But between reports (different slots), the SINR varies. So the issue is that L2SM's `linkQualityModel` computes a single effective SINR for the whole carrier, and when called 6 times with different W_sb, the SINR might be the same because the channel matrix is the same. The effective SINR depends on the precoder W and the channel H, but if the CDL-B channel at this bandwidth is nearly flat, the SINR won't vary much across subbands.

The subband SINR should theoretically differ if frequency-selective fading creates different gains in different subbands. With L2SM abstraction using the same estimated channel grid but different W_sb precoders, the fact that effective SINR stays constant suggests the channel is essentially flat in this simulation - which makes sense given the 10 MHz bandwidth, CDL-B model, and 100ns delay spread. The coherence bandwidth should be around 2 MHz, so there should be some variation, but the abstract PHY layer might be averaging across all subcarriers and symbols.

I think I've found the real issue: when `prepareLQMInput` is called for CSI-RS with different per-subband precoders, it's applying each W_sb to the full-band averaged channel estimate rather than to the frequency-selective portion of the channel for that specific subband. This means the SINR calculation doesn't actually capture per-subband channel variation - it's computing SINR for the whole grid with different precoders applied, but since the underlying channel is averaged, the results are the same.

But there's a more critical bug I'm seeing: the OLLA reset in `updateChannelQualityDL` is resetting the MCSOffset back to InitialOffset on every CSI update. If InitialOffset is zero, OLLA can never accumulate any offset value, which completely breaks the link adaptation feedback mechanism.

The constructor already initializes MCSOffset correctly, so this reset in the CSI update method is unnecessary and harmful. This explains why the BLER stays around 60% and the MCS averages too high—OLLA gets reset before it can adjust downward in response to poor channel conditions, and the scheduler occasionally sends MCS=0 on HARQ retransmissions when it shouldn't.

Now I'm identifying the remaining issues. The subband SINR calculation uses the same full-band averaged channel for each subband, so the reported values end up nearly identical unless the precoding weights differ significantly—this is more of a model limitation than a bug, but it makes the logs look suspicious. There's also an incomplete line about the sinr output that I need to see the rest of to understand what the actual issue is.

/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-4-%

/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-19-%                           applies this offset to the channel measurement
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-20-%                           based MCS. It is an integer in the range [-27,
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-21-%                           27]. The default value is 0.

/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-23-%                           packet reception fails. It is a numeric
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-24-%                           scalar in the range [0, 27]. The default value
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-25-%                           is 0.27.
5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m:26:%       StepDown          - Decremental value for the MCS offset when a
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-27-%                           packet reception is successful. It is a
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-28-%                           numeric scalar in the range [0, 27]. The
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-29-%                           default value is 0.03.

/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-37-% adopted method applies the offset directly to the MCS index.
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-38-% 
5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-39-% The adopted link adaptation algorithm consists of these steps:
5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m:40:%   1. Define the LA configuration parameters StepUp and StepDown.
5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m:41:%      The target Block error rate, TargetBLER, is defined as StepDown /
5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m:42:%      (StepDown + StepUp).
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-43-%   2. Upon receiving channel measurements feedback (CQI) from the UE node,
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-44-%      the gNB maps the reported CQI to an appropriate MCS value. The MCS
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-45-%      table used for the DL LA complies with 3GPP TS 38.214, Table
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-46-%      5.1.3.1-2.
5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m:47:%   3. Reset MCSOffset to InitialOffset on each CSI reporting
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-48-%      periodicity, considering any channel measurement errors.
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-49-%   4. Determine the MCS for physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH)
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-50-%      transmission as the MCS reported by the UE node minus the MCS offset
5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m:51:%      (PDSCH_MCS = UEReportedMCS - MCSOffset)
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-52-%   5. If the UE reports a successful PDSCH reception, decrease the
5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m:53:%      MCSOffset by StepDown.
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-54-%   6. If the UE reports a failed PDSCH reception, increase the
5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m:55:%      MCSOffset by StepUp.
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-56-%   
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-57-% Similarly, for UL link adaptation, the gNB calculates the  UL MCS
/data/part1/thangtq23/CSIRS_128T128R/5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/getMCSIndexOffset.m-58-% based on the sounding reference signal (SRS) channel measurements.

content.thinking:
Now I can see how OLLA works in the scheduler:
- `mcsOffset = fix(carrierContext.MCSOffset(...))` - this is used in MCS assignment
- `MCSIndex = min(max(mcsIndex(index) - mcsOffset, 0), 27)` - the offset is subtracted from the CQI-derived MCS

So `MCSOffset` starts at 0 (InitialOffset=0 typically), and when HARQ fails, it increases (StepUp), reducing the actual MCS sent. When HARQ succeeds, it decreases (StepDown).


The real problem is in `updateChannelQualityDL` at line 259 of nrComponentCarrierContext.m - it's resetting `MCSOffset(1)` back to `InitialOffset` every time a CSI report comes in, which happens every 5ms. This completely wipes out the accumulated OLLA adjustment that should be building up over time, so the algorithm never gets a chance to converge.

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me summarize the analysis:

---

## Phân tích log và code — Các bất thường tìm thấy

### 1. **[BUG NGHIÊM TRỌNG] OLLA (Outer-Loop Link Adaptation) bị reset mỗi 5ms**

**Triệu chứng trong log:**
- BLER ~**60% cho tất cả 64 UE** — bình thường phải ~10%
- MCS avg 25–26 dù BLER rất cao — link adaptation không phản ứng
- MCS min = 0 cho tất cả UE — HARQ retransmission liên tục

**Nguyên nhân trong code** — [nrComponentCarrierContext.m:257-260](5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/nrComponentCarrierContext.m#L257-L260):

```matlab
function updateChannelQualityDL(obj, channelQualityInfo, schedulerConfig)
    ...
    if ~isempty(schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigDL)
        % Reset link adaptation MCS offset value to initial offset  ← BUG
        obj.MCSOffset(1) = schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigDL.InitialOffset;
    end
end
```

Mỗi khi UE gửi CSI report (cứ 10 slot = 5 ms), hàm này được gọi và **reset `MCSOffset` về 0**. Trong khi đó scheduler dùng `MCSOffset` để giảm MCS khi BLER cao:

```matlab
% nrScheduler.m:458
dlAssignments(index).MCSIndex = min(max(mcsIndex(index) - mcsOffset, 0), 27);
```

OLLA tích lũy offset theo HARQ ACK/NACK (StepUp/StepDown), nhưng mỗi CSI period bị xóa trắng → MCS không bao giờ giảm → BLER mãi ở 60%.

**Fix:**
```matlab
% Xóa hoàn toàn đoạn reset này — MCSOffset chỉ được set 1 lần khi khởi tạo
% Constructor đã xử lý đúng rồi (line 229-231)
if ~isempty(schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigDL)
    % KHÔNG reset ở đây — OLLA cần tích lũy liên tục
    % obj.MCSOffset(1) = schedulerConfig.LinkAdaptationConfigDL.InitialOffset;
end
```

---

### 2. **[CẢNH BÁO] Subband SINR in log đều giống nhau**

**Triệu chứng:**
```
SINR=[31.9 31.9 31.9 31.9 31.9 31.9] dB  ← 6 subbands giống hệt nhau
```

**Nguyên nhân** — [nrUEAbstractPHY.m:279-285](5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/nrUEAbstractPHY.m#L279-L285):

```matlab
for sb = 1:numSBs
    W_sb    = pmiInfo.W(:,:,sb).';
    l2sm_sb = obj.L2SMCSI;           % value copy
    [l2sm_sb, sig_sb]  = nr5g.internal.L2SM.prepareLQMInput(l2sm_sb,
        carrierConfigInfo, csirsConfig, estChannelGrid, nVar, W_sb); % ← full-band H!
    [l2sm_sb, sinr_sb] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.linkQualityModel(l2sm_sb, sig_sb, intf);
```

Mỗi lần gọi LQM đều dùng **toàn bộ `estChannelGrid`** (average over all subcarriers/symbols), không slice theo subband. L2SM tính effective SINR trên toàn carrier → các subbands ra SINR gần như đồng nhất. Đây là giới hạn của abstract PHY, không phải lỗi logic nghiêm trọng, nhưng làm mất đi ý nghĩa của subband CQI differentiation.

---

### Tóm tắt

| # | Vị trí | Mức độ | Ảnh hưởng |
|---|--------|--------|-----------|
| 1 | `nrComponentCarrierContext.m:259` | **Nghiêm trọng** | OLLA bị phá vỡ → BLER ~60% thay vì ~10%, throughput thấp ~31% peak |
| 2 | `nrUEAbstractPHY.m:280` | Cảnh báo | Subband SINR không phân biệt theo frequency → subband PMI ít hiệu quả hơn lý thuyết |

**Ưu tiên fix Bug #1 trước** — chỉ cần xóa/comment dòng 259 trong `nrComponentCarrierContext.m`.
Sửa bug 1, giải thích kỹ hơn bug 2

content.text:
Bug #1 đã được sửa. Bây giờ giải thích chi tiết Bug #2.

---

## Giải thích chi tiết Bug #2 — Subband SINR đồng nhất

### Vấn đề cốt lõi: L2SM chỉ biết "wideband channel"

Trong abstract PHY, `nrPerfectChannelEstimate` trả về channel estimate `H` có shape:

```
H : [K × L × nRx × nTx]
     ↑   ↑
     subcarriers  symbols  (averaged across the OFDM grid)
```

Nhưng `prepareLQMInput` → `linkQualityModel` tính **một scalar EffectiveSINR duy nhất** đại diện cho toàn bộ carrier bằng cách:

1. Tính SNR matrix trên toàn bộ K subcarriers
2. Áp dụng EESM/MIESM averaging → ra **1 giá trị**

Kết quả: dù gọi 6 lần với 6 `W_sb` khác nhau, mỗi lần vẫn dùng cùng một `estChannelGrid` full-band:

```
          sb=1  sb=2  sb=3  sb=4  sb=5  sb=6
H       : same  same  same  same  same  same  (full K×L×4×128)
W_sb    : diff  diff  diff  diff  diff  diff  (chỉ cái này thay đổi)
SINR_sb : ≈same ≈same ≈same ≈same ≈same ≈same  ← vì H chiếm dominant
```

### Tại sao W_sb khác mà SINR vẫn như nhau?

Với 128T và CDL-B 10 MHz, hiệu quả SINR phụ thuộc vào:

```
SINR_eff ∝ W_sb^H · (H^H·H) · W_sb
```

- `H^H·H` là **wideband covariance** — được average over toàn bộ 24 RBs
- `W_sb` chỉ rotate trong không gian antenna, không extract per-RB channel gain
- Với CDL-B 100ns delay spread ở 10 MHz BW: coherence bandwidth ~2 MHz, tức ~6–7 RBs — channel khá flat qua 4-RB subband → per-subband covariance ≈ wideband covariance

### So sánh với những gì đúng ra phải xảy ra

| | Đúng (full PHY) | Hiện tại (abstract PHY) |
|---|---|---|
| H per subband | `H[:,sb_RBs,:,:]` — chỉ K/6 subcarriers của subband đó | Full K subcarriers |
| SINR computation | Per-subband effective SINR | Wideband SINR (scalar) |
| CQI variation | Thực sự phản ánh fading per-subband | Chỉ khác nhau do W_sb rotate khác hướng |

### Hệ quả thực tế

- **Subband CQI vẫn có biến động** (thấy rõ ở log: UE-2 slot 950 có CQI=9 trong khi slot khác CQI=15), nhưng biến động đó đến từ **sự thay đổi W_sb theo thời gian** (PMI khác slot), **không phải** từ frequency-selective fading thực sự.
- Frequency diversity gain của subband PMI bị **underestimate** — scheduler không khai thác được đúng mức frequency selectivity của kênh.
- Trong thực tế với CDL-B 100ns @ 10 MHz, gain của subband so với wideband là nhỏ, nên sai số không quá lớn.

### Fix đúng đắn (phức tạp hơn)

Cần slice `estChannelGrid` theo subband trước khi gọi LQM:

```matlab
% Trong nrUEAbstractPHY.m, vòng lặp subband:
sbRBs    = (sb-1)*sbSizeRBs + (1:sbSizeRBs);     % RB indices của subband
sbSCs    = (sbRBs - 1)*12 + (1:12*sbSizeRBs);    % subcarrier indices
H_sb     = estChannelGrid(sbSCs, :, :, :);        % slice H theo frequency
W_sb     = pmiInfo.W(:,:,sb).';

[l2sm_sb, sig_sb] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.prepareLQMInput(l2sm_sb,
    carrierConfigInfo_sb,   % ← carrier config với NSizeGrid = sbSizeRBs
    csirsConfig, H_sb, nVar, W_sb);
```

Tuy nhiên đây đòi hỏi tạo một `carrierConfigInfo_sb` với `NSizeGrid = sbSizeRBs` và `NStartGrid` tương ứng, vì `prepareLQMInput` dùng `NSizeGrid` để tính số RE. **Không trivial** và cần kiểm tra xem L2SM có hỗ trợ partial grid hay không. Với bandwidth 10 MHz (flat channel), **không cần thiết fix ngay** — ưu tiên thấp hơn Bug #1 nhiều.
"Không trivial và cần kiểm tra xem L2SM có hỗ trợ partial grid hay không." Ý là sao? Và giờ kiểm tra thử xem có gì mới?

%L2SM NR link to system mapping interface
%   NR link to system mapping interface, which implements abstraction of
%   the NR PHY.
%
%   Note: This is an internal undocumented function and its API and/or
%   functionality may change in subsequent releases.

%   The PHY abstraction models two aspects of NR PHY behavior:
%   * BLER of downlink data (PDSCH / DL-SCH) and uplink data (PUSCH / 
%     UL-SCH) transmission and reception
%   * CQI selection 
%
%   The downlink/uplink BLER model consists of the following steps:
%      1. Received bit mutual information rate (RBIR) effective SINR
%         mapping (ESM), which calculates the effective SINR based on
%         instantaneous SINRs and the symbol modulation order.
%      2. HARQ processing, including effective code rate (ECR) calculation
%         and effective SINR calculation accounting for HARQ history.
%      3. Determination of code block segment (CBS) size and number of code
%         blocks C for the TBS for the current HARQ process.
%      4. Effective SINR to code block BLER mapping: a pre-calculated SINR
%         to BLER table is selected for the current symbol modulation
%         order, ECR and CBS size, and the code block BLER is looked up
%         using the effective SINR.
%      5. The transport block BLER is calculated based on the code block
%         BLER and number of code blocks C. Decoding of the current
%         transport block is considered to have failed if a random variable
%         selected uniformly in the open interval (0,1) is lower than the
%         transport block BLER.
%
%   Step 1 is described in IEEE 802.11-14/0811r2 "Overview on RBIR-based
%   PHY Abstraction". Steps 2, 4 and 5 are described in S. Lagen, K.
%   Wanuga, H. Elkotby, S. Goyal, N. Patriciello and L. Giupponi, "New
%   Radio Physical Layer Abstraction for System-Level Simulations of 5G
%   Networks," ICC 2020 - 2020 IEEE International Conference on
%   Communications (ICC), 2020, pp. 1-7. Step 3 is described in 3GPP
%   TS 38.212 Section 5.2.2.
%
%   The CQI selection model consists of the following steps:
%   * The effective SINR is calculated as described in step 1 above.
%   * Tables from step 4 above are extracted for each MCS (i.e. each symbol
%     modulation order and ECR combination) corresponding to the entries of
%     a specified CQI table.
%   * The code block BLER for each CQI index is looked up from the tables
%     using the effective SINR, and the transport block BLER is calculated
%     as described in step 5 above.
%   * The reported CQI is established by following the procedure in TS
%     38.214 Secton 5.2.2.1 using the BLERs from the previous step.

%   Copyright 2021-2025 The MathWorks, Inc.

classdef L2SM

    methods (Static)

        % -----------------------------------------------------------------
        % Constructor
        % -----------------------------------------------------------------

        % Create variables used to store PHY abstraction state
        % syntax:
        % l2sm = L2SM(carrier);                   % for CSI-RS or SRS
        % l2sm = L2SM(...,nProcesses,nCodewords); % for PDSCH or PUSCH
        function l2sm = initialize(varargin)

            l2sm = initializeL2SM(varargin{:});

        end

        % -----------------------------------------------------------------
        % Link Quality Model (LQM) and Link Performance Model (LPM)
        % -----------------------------------------------------------------

        function [l2sm,SINRs,varargout] = linkQualityModel(varargin)

            if (nargin==6)

                % Get channel estimates in a subset of PHY channel / signal
                % locations
                [l2sm,sig,varargout{1:nargout-2}] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.prepareLQMInput(varargin{:});

                % No interference
                intf = [];

            elseif (nargin==3)

                % Get channel estimates for the signal of interest and
                % the interferers
                [l2sm,sig,intf] = deal(varargin{:});

                % Project the interferers into the allocation used by the
                % signal of interest
                intf = projectInterference(l2sm.GridSize,sig,intf);

            else % nargin==1

                % If only one input argument is provided, reset LQM cache
                l2sm = varargin{1};
                [l2sm,~,~,~,varargout{1:nargout-2}] = getChannelEstimates(l2sm);
                SINRs = [];
                return;

            end

            % Calculate SINRs using IEEE 802.11 methodology
            SINRs = calculateSINR(sig,intf);

        end

        function [l2sm,sig,varargout] = prepareLQMInput(l2sm,carrier,chsig,estChannelGrid,noiseEst,wtx)

            % Get channel estimates in a subset of PHY channel / signal 
            % locations
            [l2sm,hest,prgsubs,sinrsubs,varargout{1:nargout-2}] = getChannelEstimates(l2sm,carrier,chsig,estChannelGrid,wtx);
            
            % Record channel estimates and other information in a
            % convenience structure
            sig.sinrsubs = sinrsubs;
            sig.hest = hest;
            sig.prgsubs = prgsubs;
